# circhelp

`circhelp` is a small helper package for circular data analyses in R,
particularly useful for cognitive studies on orientation, motion
direction, and other circular features. The package contains functions
for descriptive statistics of circular data (computing means, SD, and
skewness), angular differences, and correlation. It also includes a
function to correct for cardinal biases in human estimates of circular
features (e.g., orientation). The documentation is available in the
package help files and at <https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/>.

## Installation

You can install the latest released version from CRAN with:

``` r
install.packages("circhelp")
```

The current developmental version from [GitHub](https://github.com/) can
be installed with:

``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("achetverikov/circhelp")
```

## Usage

Most functions are self-explanatory.

``` r
library(circhelp)
library(mgcv)
#> Loading required package: nlme
#> This is mgcv 1.9-3. For overview type 'help("mgcv-package")'.
# compute a set of descriptive statistics
x <- rnorm(500)
circ_descr(x)
#> $mu
#> [1] 0.01289167
#> 
#> $sigma
#> [1] 0.9319964
#> 
#> $skew_pewsey
#> [1] -0.009480068
#> 
#> $skew_fischer
#> [1] -0.06900768
#> 
#> $rho
#> [1] 0.6477123
#> 
#> $skew_rel_to_zero
#> [1] -0.004524587

# compute difference in orientations
a <- 5
b <- 170
angle_diff_180(a, b)
#> [1] 15

# compute difference in 360° space (e.g., motion directions)
angle_diff_360(a, b)
#> [1] -165

# compute correlation between angles
data <- rmvn(10000, c(0, 0), V = matrix(c(1, 0.5, 0.5, 1), ncol = 2))
circ_corr(data[, 1], data[, 2])
#> [1] 0.452809
```

The only (somewhat) complicated function is `remove_cardinal_biases`,
see the [help
files](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/reference/remove_cardinal_biases.html)
and [the
vignette](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/articles/cardinal_biases.html)
for an example use case. ![Three side-by-side scatter plots showing
response errors in a circular orientation task. Left panel: raw errors
versus stimulus orientation, with two orientation bins in blue and green
and curved trend lines showing systematic bias near cardinal
orientations. Middle panel: same data after correcting for bias, with
errors centered closer to zero. Right panel: estimated bias values for
each orientation, with circles for regular points and triangles marking
outliers.](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/articles/cardinal_biases_files/figure-html/correct_biases_ex-1.png "Example of cardinal biases processing")

# Package index

## Descriptive statistics

- [`circ_mean_rad()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_mean_rad.md)
  [`circ_mean_180()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_mean_rad.md)
  [`circ_mean_360()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_mean_rad.md)
  : Circular mean
- [`circ_sd_rad()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_sd_rad.md)
  [`circ_sd_360()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_sd_rad.md)
  [`circ_sd_180()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_sd_rad.md)
  : Circular standard deviation
- [`smoothed_circ_sd()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/smoothed_circ_sd.md)
  : Compute smoothed estimate of circular standard deviation
- [`circ_descr()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_descr.md)
  : A set of descriptive statistics for circular data
- [`weighted_circ_mean()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/weighted_circ_mean.md)
  [`weighted_circ_mean2()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/weighted_circ_mean.md)
  [`weighted_circ_sd()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/weighted_circ_mean.md)
  [`weighted_circ_rho()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/weighted_circ_mean.md)
  : Weighted circular parameters
- [`weighted_sem()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/weighted_sem.md)
  : Weighted standard error of the mean (SEM_w)

## Cardinal biases

- [`remove_cardinal_biases()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/remove_cardinal_biases.md)
  : Remove cardinal biases
- [`remove_cardinal_biases_discrete()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/remove_cardinal_biases_discrete.md)
  : Remove cardinal biases for data with orientation (color, motion,
  ...) set in discrete steps

## Plotting

- [`circ_loess()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_loess.md)
  :

  An implementation of circular-linear locally-weighted regression
  (LOESS)

## Miscellanous

- [`angle_diff_rad()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/angle_diff_rad.md)
  [`angle_diff_360()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/angle_diff_rad.md)
  [`angle_diff_180()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/angle_diff_rad.md)
  [`angle_diff_90()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/angle_diff_rad.md)
  [`angle_diff_180_45()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/angle_diff_rad.md)
  [`angle_diff_360_90()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/angle_diff_rad.md)
  : Differences between angles in different circular spaces
- [`vm_kappa_to_circ_sd()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/vm_kappa_to_circ_sd.md)
  [`vm_kappa_to_circ_sd_deg()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/vm_kappa_to_circ_sd.md)
  [`vm_circ_sd_to_kappa()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/vm_kappa_to_circ_sd.md)
  [`vm_circ_sd_deg_to_kappa()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/vm_kappa_to_circ_sd.md)
  : Conversion between the circular SD and kappa of von Mises
- [`correct_angle_rad()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/correct_angle_rad.md)
  : Get angle value in \[-pi, pi\] space
- [`pad_circ()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/pad_circ.md)
  : Pad circular data on both ends
- [`circ_corr()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_corr.md)
  : Circular correlation coefficient
- [`circ_lin_corr()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/circ_lin_corr.md)
  : Circular-linear correlation
- [`density_asymmetry()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/density_asymmetry.md)
  : Compute asymmetry in weighted probability density
- [`density_asymmetry_discrete()`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/density_asymmetry_discrete.md)
  : Compute asymmetry in weighted probability density for discrete data

## Datasets

- [`Bae_Luck_2018_data`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/Bae_Luck_2018_data.md)
  : Data from a motion estimation task
- [`Pascucci_et_al_2019_data`](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/reference/Pascucci_et_al_2019_data.md)
  : Data from an orientation estimation task

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Correcting for cardinal biases to improve serial dependence
  estimates](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/articles/cardinal_biases.md):
- [Estimating serial dependence with
  density_asymmetry()](https://achetverikov.github.io/circhelp/index.html/articles/serial_dependence_with_density_asymmetry.md):
